The v2 xDS API was deprecated and replaced by the v3 API starting with Envoy 1.19+. The inventory includes: (1) 35 core v2 API proto files in api/envoy/api/v2/ defining cluster, endpoint, listener, and route discovery services (CDS, EDS, LDS, RDS); (2) 11 v2 API subcategory proto files covering authentication, core transport, cluster filters, and rate limiting configurations; (3) 15+ v2-based configuration proto files in api/envoy/config/ covering listener, trace, and various filter configurations; (4) 6 v2 service discovery proto files in api/envoy/service/discovery/v2; (5) Additional v2-based service proto files for accesslog, tracing, metrics, rate limiting, and load statistics; (6) C++ source and header files that include v2 protobuf definitions; and (7) Corresponding duplicate proto definitions in the data-plane-api repository. These proto files define the package namespaces (envoy.api.v2, envoy.api.v2.auth, envoy.api.v2.core, etc.) and contain references to v2 resource types like envoy.api.v2.Cluster, envoy.api.v2.Listener, envoy.api.v2.ClusterLoadAssignment. The proto files include migration annotations indicating v3 migration paths. C++ tests and configuration validation files reference v2 proto headers through includes like #include 'envoy/api/v2/cds.pb.h' and string references to v2 service names like 'envoy.api.v2.RouteDiscoveryService'. These files represent the deprecated v2 API surface that should be evaluated for migration to v3 as part of the Envoy 1.31 lifecycle."
